notes: Local workstation measurements include hardware and scheduler variance.
UDS claims require UDS transport measurements.
Claims are evaluated against March 2026 whitepaper text.

claims.quote: The bridge round-trip for a typical agent message (under 1KB of state) adds 0.1-0.3ms of latency.
The Unix domain socket bridge supports 50,000-100,000 messages per second.
Booting the Elixir runtime adds 500-1000ms to application startup.
Supervisors restart child processes when they fail with one_for_one, one_for_all, and rest_for_one semantics.
claims.threshold: validated if ratio >= 0.99 and all checks pass
claims.partial_rule: partial if ratio >= 0.90 and 2/3 checks pass
claims.caveats: Bridge recovery checks over Elixir runtime.

claims.quote: The Pyre bridge adds approximately 143MB of base memory and each agent on the Elixir side consumes approximately 2.9KB.
claims.threshold: validated if base in [100,180]MB and slope in [2,5]KB
claims.partial_rule: partial if base in [80,200]MB and slope <= 10KB
claims.caveats: Absolute RSS is verdict source; idle-BEAM delta is contextual.

claims.quote: No preemption within Python: CPU-intensive handler work can block the Python worker/event loop.
Developers write pure Python and interact with Python APIs.
claims.threshold: validated if public Python runtime APIs are available and documented
claims.partial_rule: partial if APIs exist but docs/tests missing

claims.quote: State is serialized across the bridge and constrained to serializable models.
claims.threshold: validated if BaseModel enforcement + codec tests present
claims.partial_rule: partial if only one evidence source present

claims.quote: Bridge/runtime observability provides lifecycle and error event visibility.
claims.threshold: validated if event API + integration tests exist
claims.partial_rule: partial if API exists without tests
claims.caveats: Evidence from health event hooks and tests.

claims.quote: Distributed clustering for cross-machine agents is a future direction.
Hot code reloading for Python handlers is a future direction.
Streaming bridge message types are a future direction.
WASM-based isolation as an alternative is a future direction.
claims.caveats: No WASM execution backend in current repository.

claim_verdicts.detail: future/distributed claim outside local single-node validation scope
future/distributed claim outside local single-node validation scope
future/distributed claim outside local single-node validation scope
future/distributed claim outside local single-node validation scope
blockers: No WASM execution backend in current repository.
